servinfo.title: Rear Brake Disc Removal and Installation

attention4.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions during rear brake caliper removal may cause damage to the rear brake flexible hose and/or the brake piston.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the rear brake flexible hose during rear brake caliper removal.
Avoid depressing the brake pedal during the brake disc removal procedure to prevent the brake piston from being forced out of the cylinder.

s1.ptxt: Hoist vehicle and remove rear wheel.
Remove rear brake caliper carrier bolts (1) and remove rear brake caliper carrier (2) from rear axle.
Push out rear brake disc (2) by tightening two M8 bolts (1) evenly into jack bolt holes.

attention4.ptxt: If the rear brake flexible hose is twisted, the hose will be damaged.
If the hose is twisted, reinstall rear brake caliper being careful not to let rear brake flexible hose get twisted.

s1.ptxt: Install rear brake caliper carrier (1) to rear axle by tightening brake caliper carrier bolts to specified torque.
Depress brake pedal three times or more with engine running to activate pad clearance adjustment mechanism so that clearance between rear brake disc and rear brake pads will be appropriate.
Check that brakes are not dragging and that appropriate braking force can be obtained.
Install rear wheel and check that parking brake lever stroke is appropriate and parking brake produces proper braking force.
